evidence_quotes.quote: the internet in its current state is more dark pattern than actual good internet.
In game monitization should seriously be looked at deeply and honestly *deserves it's own act*.

paragraphs.text: Seeing the existance of this act is yet another good step the EU has made, atleast one of the good ones, in the pursuit of making the internet a lot more fair. However, I would love if there was extra care given to this act. the internet in its current state is more dark pattern than actual good internet. Specifically I think extra care should be given to services that force you into an algorithim that is anti consumer. examples of this are: Twitter/X's algorithm being tuned to make people react leading to them seeing content that they would not want to see because it makes them angry and thus react. Youtube adding a bunch of carosells when searching giving unreleated results, meaning more than 50% of the results you're looking for are obscured by the algorithm. Not only that but the fact you cannot disable these or youtube shorts, having to go out of your way to use third party content blockers to be able too avoid addictive content. Short form content apps (youtube shorts, tiktok, reels) creating an algorithm that shows you content that keeps you addicted to the app instead of content that you want to watch. I.e. say you want good cooking tips but tiktok notices you stay longer with escalator fails. It will show the latter rather than the former even if the latter is not what you really want. All of these fall under dark patterns intended to unfairly force consumers into a consuming to either gain an unfair advantage or worse, make their users addicted and worse off. Furthermore, this act should also address SEO abuse. As this is one of the leading factors in both creating google's monopoly on search but are also a leading farm in dark patterns. For example. if you search up: "Best VPN 2025" on google, after ignoring the AI recap, you will find articles listing good vpns. However 9/10 of those websites are either owners of said vpns or are sponsored by them and mimic real articles to get top SEO results. This has led a huge chunk of internet users to add "reddit" to their search results. They do this to bypass the SEO and get real answers and results. Problem is, in response google *bought* the exclusive rights to index reddit. meaning they now have a monopoly on a workaround of a problem that prays on dark patterns. So they profitted from a massive dark pattern and absusive practice. Now shifting gears, video games. In game monitization should seriously be looked at deeply and honestly *deserves it's own act*. That said I hope the foundation to kill premium curencies, games designed around boring gameplay that can be removed by constant purchases (usually designated as freemium or gacha games), can be layed upon with this act. And as many people are saying thanks to a movement that hijacked this feedback forum. A foundation against killing games would be nice to see in this act as well. I hope that my feedback shines a light on some particularly important things. I worry it may get drowned out but a shot not shot is always a miss.
